rationale: The citing paper is about fairness ideals and QRE estimation in a manufacturer–retailer pricing game, while the cited paper is a Bayesian conjoint-methods study on imputing missing attribute levels in partial profiles. With only a bare DOI match in the references (no contextual citation text) and no overlap in constructs, data, or methods described in the notes, this looks like an incidental/background mention rather than a substantive dependency.
